title: Failure Classification, Retry Budgets, Backoff, and Load Shedding · Mastery Check
summary: A production review compares near-miss TypeScript messaging decisions for failure classification, retry budgets, backoff, and load shedding and requires the smallest defensible failure taxonomy and bounded retry policy with changed-case evidence. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for failure taxonomy and bounded retry policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify malformed, unauthorized, missing dependency, rate limit, timeout, conflict, transient, permanent, and ambiguous effect failures by evidence.
Bound attempts, total age, delay, processing deadline, queue residence, and operator escalation as one retry budget.
Apply exponential backoff and jitter without blocking consumer slots or requeueing hot loops and preserve intended availability time.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
